transcript.text: This is Jack Harris. I'm co-founder of Health and Marijuana Prohibition.
In 1973, Captain Ed Pagare, who owned a counterculture store in San Fernando Valley, became my mentor.
And we learned from the kids that would hang around there that marijuana is also known as a plant called hemp.
And I thought, well, that was interesting. One night, Captain Ed and I stumbled upon an idea that hemp was the number one source of paper-fiber-fuel food on Earth of the 300 plants.
Forget smoking it. But historically, this plant had been known since time immemorial as man's number one plant.
All the paper. I don't eat paper. It was made from marijuana. It was made from a plant called hemp.
I never knew that virtually all clothing, all bedsheets, all homespun, that famous American homespun, was all marijuana. 85-90%.
Was it wool? It wasn't cotton. It wasn't Judas Isler flags. It was from marijuana.
All this material education. I went from public school, home school, and to college.
That was a few 10,000 years right up until this century.
And there was a number one thing known by kids a hundred years ago that went to school.
I used a dictionary, an encyclopedia, and the United States Government Department of Ag Culture, because it's all there.
One is written about cannabis, hemp. Then all the other plants that we have, wheat and corn, barley and rice,
Normally written about hemp, hemp is that important. It was virtually all our clothing.
There are no known deaths from marijuana. You live longer according to the Jamaican study,
Because the Swedish study is funded by the United States Government, and the American study is funded by the United States Government.
